tldr: These tools serve different purposes — Rekognition is a vision API, FormatJS is an open-source i18n library — so this is a workflow change, not a 1:1 migration.
why_switch: FormatJS is free and open source with no vendor lock-in.
Standards-based ICU MessageFormat support for pluralization and rich formatting.
Strong React ecosystem integration via react-intl.
No per-request billing or quota concerns.
Better fit if your real need is application-level internationalization rather than image analysis.
what_to_export: Existing translation strings and message catalogs (if any)
Any AWS-side text or label outputs being shown to users
Pluralization and date/number formatting rules in use
Any custom translation memory or glossaries
Sample UI screens for QA in each locale
export_method: If you have no existing translation pipeline, start by extracting hardcoded user-facing strings from your codebase. If translations exist elsewhere (spreadsheets, another platform), export them in a structured format such as JSON or CSV keyed by message ID. Consult FormatJS official docs for the recommended message format.
import_method: Install the FormatJS toolchain (e.g., @formatjs/cli, react-intl or equivalent runtime) and convert exported strings into ICU MessageFormat message files per locale. Wire up the IntlProvider at the app root, replace hardcoded strings with FormattedMessage components or the intl.formatMessage API, and run the FormatJS extractor to keep catalogs in sync. Consult FormatJS official docs for setup specifics.

concept_mapping.note: Configuration is now build-time, not request-time.
Conceptually replaces 'unsure' handling with a fallback strategy.
All formatting happens client/server-side via library.

effort: 4-12 hours for a small app, longer if string extraction is greenfield
gotchas: FormatJS does not perform translation — you still need translators or an MT service.
ICU MessageFormat has a learning curve for plurals and selectors.
Bundle size grows with locale data; lazy-load locale data when possible.
If you genuinely need image analysis, FormatJS is not a replacement — keep Rekognition or pick a vision tool.
SSR setups (Next.js, Remix) require careful provider placement.

email.subject: Migrating from Amazon Rekognition to FormatJS
email.body:
Hi team,

Note that Amazon Rekognition (vision API) and FormatJS (i18n library) cover different problems, so this isn't a like-for-like migration. If our actual need is internationalization, the plan is to adopt FormatJS, extract user-facing strings into ICU message catalogs, and wire up IntlProvider in the app. Estimated effort: a few hours to a couple of days depending on string volume. If we still need image analysis, we should keep that tool separately.

Thanks
